def: A night so called by the Hindūs.
s.m. Neighing, neigh (of a horse).
adj. & s.m. Of or belonging to the sect of ʻAlī; — a follower of the sect of 'Alī ( = shīʻa).
more: s. A follower of the sect of aﻌlī: see شيعه (Pers. plur. شيعيان).

ety: imperat. & rt. of shustan; fr. Zend śud = S. शुध्
def: part. adj. & s.m. Washing; — washer (used as last member of compounds).
more: part. act. (from شستن) in compos. Washing, washer. ¶ shau (contract. of شوهر) s. m. Husband.
wety: Borrowed from English show.

ety: v.n. of شعّ 'to be scattered or dispersed'
def: s.f. The rays or beams of the sun, sunbeams, sunshine, radiance, light, splendour, lustre.
more: n. f. شع dispersed. The rays of the sun; sunshine; radiance.
(from شعّ To radiate, be spread or diffused) s. f. Light, splendour, lustre, the rays of the sun, sunshine, radiance.

def: adj. Of or relating to the sun's rays, full of rays, radial, radiant.
more: adj. Of or relating to the sunʼs rays, of a radius, radial.

ety: fr. shustan; see sho or shū
def: s.m. Washing; a wash.

ety: for A. شعبة shǒʻbat, v.n. fr. شعب 'to collect;' 'to separate,' &c.
def: s.m. A crack, cleft in a mountain; a watercourse, a channel in which water flows; a branch; a ramification, a division.
more: s. m. The cleft or crack of a mountain in which water stands, a channel in which water flows especially through the sand.

ety: for A. شعبذة shaʻbaẕat, inf. n. of شعبذ; or شعوذة, inf. n. of شعوذ, 'to practise legerdemain'
def: s.m. Legerdemain, sleight of hand, juggling, conjuration
subs: s.m. One who practises legerdemain, a juggler, conjurer
s.f. Sleight of hand, conjuring, juggling trick, jugglery, hocus-pocus.

ety: for A. شعبذة shaʻbaẕat, inf. n. of شعبذ; or شعوذة, inf. n. of شعوذ, 'to practise legerdemain'
def: s.m. Legerdemain, sleight of hand, juggling, conjuration
more: n. m. Sleight of hand; juggle.
(improp. shaﻌbada ) s. m. Sleight of hand, conjuration. سعبده باز shuﻌbada-bāz, s. m. A juggler, a conjurer. شعبده بازي shuﻌbada-bāz-ī, s. f. Conjuration, sleight of hand, juggling, hocus-pocus.
subs: s.m. One who practises legerdemain, a juggler, conjurer
s.f. Sleight of hand, conjuring, juggling trick, jugglery, hocus-pocus.

def: n. m. A profligate; a bad or loose character (بدمعاش). 1. Shohde, luchche, muë khudāī ke, Phiṭṭe-muṅh aisī be-hayāī ke! Wom. Shauq. Out on you profligate, loose wretch, godless! Accursed be of Heaven such shamelessness! 2. Mue shode ke kaun muṅh lage? Mah. Wom. Who would have anything to do with such an abandoned wretch? shohdā-pan, shohd-pan, shod-panā , n. m. Dissoluteness (بدمعاشي). shod-pan phailānā, yā k. , v. a. To play the part of a scoundrel.

n. f. shohra , n. m. Celebrity shohrat paidā k. , v. n. To acquire a name; become famous. shohrat denā , v. a. To give currency to; proclaim; blazon ( ishtehār d ). shohrat h. , v. n. See dhūm h. , 2. shohra-i-āfāq , adj. World-famous.

ety: act. part. of shoʼīdan; see sho or shū
def: part. adj. Washing (used as last member of compounds).
more: part. act. (from شوييدن) in compos. Washing.

def: adj. Bright (as colour, &c.), cheerful, gay, sprightly; buxom; brisk, spirited; humorous, sly; whimsical, capricious, playful, mischievous; petulant, saucy, bold, daring, hardened, strong; insolent, presumptuous; brazen, wanton, impudent, shameless; — s.f. A wanton
more: adj. 1. Mischievous ( burā , 7). 2. ( Poet .) Saucy; pert; playful; piquant. 3. Bright. Shokh rang . 4. Wanton; hold. shokh-chashm, yā dīdah , adj. Wanton-eyed. shokh-chashmī , n. f. Wantonness.

adj. Mischievous, playful, pert, saucy, brisk, cheerful, buxom, sly, humorous, wanton, strong, insolent, presumptuous. شوخ چشم shoḵẖ-chashm, adj. Wanton-eyed, having a playful eye, unceremonious. شوخ چشمي shoḵẖ-chashm-ī, s. f. Wantonness or playfulness of the eye.
subs: or shoḵẖ-dīda, adj. Playful-eyed; of a wanton look, lewd, impudent
s.f. Playfulness of the eye; wantonness

def: s.f. Playfulness, fun, mischief; pertness, sauciness; coquetry, wantonness; forwardness, boldness, insolence, &c.
more: n. f. 1. Mischief (شرارت). 2. Sauciness; pertness. 3. Wantonness; boldness. 1. Haṅs denā har dam nāz-bharā, dikhlānā saj dhaj shokhī kā, Har gāli misrī, qand bharī, har ek qadam aṭkhelī kā, Dil shād kyā aur moh līyā, yeh joban pāyā Holī ne! Nazir. Coquettish laughter with such saucy airs and graces shewn, Abuse as candy sweet, each step the sportive wanton's own: The heart entranced, with pleasure fed, such charms in Holi known! 2. Chhiṛaknā raṅg khūbāṅ par ajab shokhī dikhātā hai, Kabhī kuchh nāz kī 'voh voh,' kabhī andāz 'rah rah' kā . Holī. Nazīr. Pink water squirted on the fair. such pretty airs they show, Now such coquettish ' oh, oh, ohs!' now pretty ' hold, hold ,' do! shokhī se , adv. 1. Saucily. 2. Wantonly. shokhī k. , v. a. To be naughty, saucy.
s. f. Mischief, playfulness, fun, pertness, forwardness, humour, sauciness, wantonness, coquetry.
subs: adv. Playfully, in mischief; pertly; wantonly, &c.
To practise mischief, &c.; to be pert or saucy; to be insolent; to be wanton, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوخِی (šōxī).

ety: for A. شعلة shǒʻlat, v.n. fr. شعل 'to burn, blaze,' &c.
def: s.m. Flame, blaze, light, flash
more: n. m. A flame. shola-khū , Poet. adj. Hot; fiery. shola-rū , Poet. n. m. A blazing beauty. sholā bhabūkā h. , v. n. To be inflamed; to burn with rage.

(from شعل To kindle fire) s. m. Flame, blaze, light, flash. شعله افشان shuﻌla-afshān or شعله فشان shuﻌla-fishān, Scattering flame, flame-scatterer. شعله بار shuﻌla-bār, adj. Raining flames. شعله پيشه shuﻌla-pesha, adj. Flame in practice, burning as a flame. شعله خو shuﻌla-ḵẖū, adj. Fiery in disposition. شعله خوئي shuﻌla-ḵẖū-ī, s. f. Fieriness of disposition. شعله خيز shuﻌla-ḵẖez, adj. Flame-exciting. شعله خيزي shuﻌla-ḵẖez-ī, s. f. The rising up or exciting of flame. شعله رخ shuﻌla-ruḵẖ, or شعله رو shuﻌla-rū, adj. Flame-faced, having a bright countenance. شعله زن shuﻌla-zan, adj. Blazing, flashing, brilliant.
subs: adj. & s.m. Dispersing or scattering flame; — flame-scatterer
adj. & s.m. Constantly setting aflame or ablaze; burning as a flame; — one whose regular business it is to set aflame or ablaze
adj. Fiery in disposition, fiery, hot
adj. Raising a flame, flame-exciting, making a blaze
or shǒʻla-rū, adj. & s.m. Flame-faced, having a bright countenance; a blazing beauty
adj. Blazing, flashing, flaming, brilliant, resplendent
or shǒʻla-bhabūkā honā, v.n. To be inflamed, to burn with rage.
ex: یہ سچ کہ بھڑکتے شعلوں سے دامن کو بچانا کھیل نہیں
It is true that to [try and] save a soul from a blazing fire is no easy task
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شُعْلَه (šu'la), borrowed from Arabic شُعْلَة (šuʕla). First attested in c. 1609 as Middle Hindi شعلہ (ś'lh).

def: s.m. corr. of shora, q.v. adj. & s.m. Barren (ground); marshy; — barren or nitrous soil; — saltpetre; nitre; nitrate of potash
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of saltpetre, made of saltpetre

def: s.m. A dish of rice and pulse boiled together till quite soft; soft khićṛī, q.v.
more: n. m. Cor. of shulla liquid. A dish of rice and pulse boiled soft together and given to sick persons; soft khichṛī .
(corrupt. from شله shula ) s. m. A dish consisting of rice and pulse boiled together and given to sick persons.
s. m. A dish, khichṛī with meat ( Qanoon-e-islam ).

ety: prob. shūm, q.v.+Prk. आलिओ, and आलिआ = S. आल+इकः, and इका
def: adj. (dialec.) Slothful, lazy, indolent ( = sust); — s.f. Sloth, indolence ( = sustī).
more: (dakh) 1. adj. Slothful, lazy, indolent. 2. s. f. Sloth, indolence: see سستي sust-ī.

def: s.m. Cry, noise, outcry, exclamation, din, clamour, uproar, tumult, disturbance; renown; — adj. Disturbed (in mind), mad ( = shorīda); — salt, brackish (cf. S. kshāra); very bitter; — unlucky
more: n. m. Cry; outcry; din; clamour ( dhūm , 1). shor k., yā machānā , v. a. To make a noise, uproar, row.

n. m. A saline substance; anatron. shor , adj. Salt; brackish. shor zamīn ; H. kallhaṛ , E. usal , n. f. Land impregnated with saltpetre; saline soil. Dekho, unkī chīz na chheṛo! uṭheṅge to shor kareṅge. shor, yā khār lagnā , v. n. To become saline.

1. s. m. Cry, noise, outcry, clamour, din, renown, uproar, exclamation, disturbance, tumult. 2. adj. Salt, brackish, very bitter. 3. adj. Unlucky. 4. part. act. (of شوريدن) Disturbed, mad. شورانگيز shor-angez, Exciting tumults. شورآور shor-āwar, adj. Tumultuous, noisy, calamitous. شوربخت shor-baḵẖt, adj. Unfortunate, wretched, infamous. شوربور shor-bor (see شرابور) Very wet, drenched. شورپشت shor-pusht, adj. Quarrelsome, encouraging disturbance, noisy. شورچشم shor-chashm, adj. Having an unlucky or malignant eye. شورزمين shor-zamīn, s. f. Salsuginous ground, barren land. شورشار shor-shār or شورشر shor-sharr, or شورشرابا shor-sharābā, or شورشغب shor-shag̠ẖab, s. m. Noise, tumult, disturbance, bustle, clamour. شورمچانا shor-machānā, To make a noise, to hoot.
subs: (or shora-pusht), adj. Noisy, quarrelsome, encouraging disturbance, refractory, unruly
adj. Having an unlucky or malignant eye
or shor-sharābā, or shor-shag̠ab, s.m. Noise, bustle, clamour, tumult, disturbance
v.n. To make a noise, create an uproar or disturbance, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شور (šōr).

def: adj. & s.m. Barren (ground); marshy; — barren or nitrous soil; — saltpetre; nitre; nitrate of potash
more: 1. adj. Marshy, barren (ground). 2. s. m. Nitre, saltpetre. شوره گر shora-gar, s. m. A manufacturer of saltpetre.
subs: adj. (f. -ī), Of saltpetre, made of saltpetre

def: n. f. Refractoriness; turbulence.
s.m. Soup, broth.
more: n. m. Broth; soup. Ginī boṭī (ḍalī), napā shurvā . Prov. Counted each bit of meat with measured helps of soup. (Rigid economy).
wety: From Classical Persian شوربا (šōrbā).

def: s. (dakh.) Broth: see شوربا shorbā.
adj. = sharābor, q.v.

ety: perf. part. of shorīdan, fr. shor, q.v.
def: part. adj. Disturbed (in mind), distracted, mad, frantic; desperately in love; faint; dejected
more: part. pass. (of شوريدن) 1. Disturbed, mad, desperately in love. 2. Faint, dejected. شوريده حال shorīda-ḥāl, adj. Mad, &c. شوريده خاطر shorīda-ḵẖāt̤ir, adj. Dejected, melancholy.
subs: adj. In a distracted state (of mind), demented, mad, &c.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شوریده (šōrīda).

def: s.f. Derangement, distractedness, madness (from love); love, insane passion.
s.f. Commotion, confusion, tumult, disturbance, insurrection, &c.; — brackishness, saltness
more: n. f. Disturbance, etc. ( balvā ). shorish barpā k. , v. a. To excite an insurrection.
(from شوريدن) s. f. 1. Tumult, insurrection, confusion. 2. Brackishness, saltness.
subs: To create a disturbance, excite an insurrection.

def: s.f. Saline quality; brackishness, saltness.
s.m. = shorbā, q.v. s.m. Soup, broth.
more: s. (dakh.) Broth ( Harris ): see شوربا shorbā.

def: s.m. An ingot (of gold or silver); a bar (of iron, &c.); a chip; a particle, part, bit, piece; rubbish, filth.
more: s. m. 1. An ingot. 2. Chips, rubbish, filth. 3. A particle, a part.

ety: for A. شعشعة, inf. n. of شعشع 'to make thin and long'
def: s.m. A long thin stroke, a stroke; tail of a letter); a cedilla; — fiction, hoax, false report
more: n. m. 1. Fiction ( ushgula ). 2. A stroke. He kā shoshā . 3. A cedilla. shoshā uṭhānā, yā chhoṛnā , v. a. To spread a false report. Hazrat ne āte hī nayā shoshā chhoṛā . No sooner come His Highness lies invents. shoshā denā , v. a. 1. To make a stroke. 2. To excite (اُکسانا 6.).
subs: or shoshā ćhoṛnā, v.n. To spread a false report, &c.
(-ko), To make a stroke (to); to excite.

def: n. m. Funeral obsequies. It consists chiefly in offering cakes ( pinḍa ), water, etc. to deceased ancestors before a sacrificial fire.
s.m. pl. (of shāʻir), Poets.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شعرا (šu'arā), from Arabic شُعَرَاء (šuʕarāʔ).

def: s.m. corr. of shubha, q.v. s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust
wety: From Sanskrit शुभ (śubha).

def: s.m. A shepherd, pastor.

ety: for A. شبہة shubhat, v.n. fr. شبه; ii, شبّه 'to render confused or dubious,' &c.
def: s.m. Confusedness, dubiousness, doubt, uncertainty, ambiguity; hesitation, suspicion; suspense; question, scruple; a defect (esp. in law), a flaw; presumption (of guilt)
more: (from شبه To liken, to compare, dissemble, render uncertain) s. m. Doubt, suspicion, ambiguity, hesitation.
subs: (-kā), To entertain doubt (of), to doubt; to suspect, distrust
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شبهه (šubha), from Arabic شُبْهَة (šubha), from verb شَبَّهَ (šabbaha, “to render confused”).

def: adv. So so.

ety: perf. part. of shudan; and = Zend shūta; S. च्युत
def: part. Become, having become; past, gone; lost
more: part. pass. (of شدن) Become, having become, gone, lost.
subs: adv. Going on (from one thing to another) gradually; by degrees, gradually (syn. hote hote).

def: s. (dakh.) See شـﮩدا shuhdā.
adv. By degrees.
s.f. A going and coming; custom, usage.

ety: rt. Zend shu = S. च्यु; — dan = S. aff. तं
def: inf. & gerund, To become; becoming (used in comp.).
more: inf. or verbal noun, in compos. To become, becoming.

def: adj. Fit to be or become; what is to be; probable, possible; practicable; — s.f. A coming to pass, an occurrence.
more: n. f. An occurrence (بهاوي and اتفاق 8). shudanī , adj. What is to be. Shudnī amar ho-kar rahtā hai. nā-shudanī , adj. Unpromising ( bad-bakht ).
s. f. Occurrence, coming to pass. adj. Practicable, possible.

def: s.f. Circumstances, particulars; slight knowledge.
more: n. f. A little knowledge. shud bud h. , v. n. To have some little knowledge.

def: adv. So so; indifferently.

adj. S. शुद्ध 1. Right ( ṭhīk , 3). Shudh bolo . 2. Pure. (pāk 1—4). 3. Shaving the head on the death of a relation. 4. ( Kāyasths ) Meat; flesh. shudh-patr ; A. sehat-nāmah , n. m. Corrigenda. shudh k. , v. a. 1. To correct 2. To purify. ashudh-patr ; A. galat-nāmah , n. m. Errata.
ex: ہِنْدوسْتانِی زُبان میں شُدّھ قُرْآن کے بولِیاں.
hindostānī zubān mẽ śuddh qur-ān ke boliyā̃.
Speeches of sacred Quran in Hindustani language.
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit शुद्ध (śuddha), from the root verb शुध् (śudh, “to become pure or purified”).

def: s.m. Ground tilled and sown; — a person whose business it is to examine the state of the harvest; — verification, examination; estimation, valuation, survey.
more: s. m. 1. Ground tilled and sown. 2. A person whose business it is to examine the state of the harvest. 3. Survey, valuation, estimate.

ety: for A. شفعة shufʻat, v.n. fr. شفع 'to join to, to couple,' &c.
def: s.m. Any possession coveted; the right of obtaining possession (of a piece of land, &c.), the right of pre-emption, pre-emption
more: (from شفع) s. m. Any possession coveted. حق شفع ḥaḳḳ-i-shufʼaﻌ, Right of preemption in favour of a person whose possessions adjoin to a land offered for sale.
subs: s.m. The right of pre-emption in favour of a person whose possessions adjoin to a land offered for sale.

def: n. m. شفع joined. Preemption. shuf'ٴi , n. m. One who has a right of preemption.
s.m. = shugūn, or śagun, q.v.
more: (improp. shagun ) s. m. Omen, &c. See شگون shugūn.
wety: Semi-learned borrowing from Sanskrit शकुन (śakuna).

def: s.m. An omen, augury ( = śagun); — betrothal.
more: (improp. shagūn. Sans. शकुन ) s. m. Augury, omen.

def: s.m. = shugūniyā, q.v.
s.m. An explainer of omens, an augur, a diviner, soothsayer; a fortune-teller.
more: s. m. A diviner, an augur, an explainer of omens.

def: s.m. = shugūniyā, q.v.
s.m. pl. (of shahīd), Martyrs.
more: s. m. A rake, prodigal, debauchee, blackguard, scoundrel, vagabond. شہدا شکسته shuhdā-shikasta, Ruined and wretched.
wety: Borrowed from Arabic شُهَدَاء (šuhadāʔ).

ety: for A. شہرة, inf. n. of شہر 'to be apparent, or conspicuous,' &c.
def: adj. Of world-wide celebrity, renowned
more: s. m. Fame, renown, reputation, divulging, publishing, report, rumour. شہره آفاق shuhra-e-āfāḳ, or شہره عالم shuhra-e-āﻌlam, Renowned, known over the world.
subs: (-kī), To acquire a reputation (for); to become famous
(-ko), To give publicity (to), to proclaim, to blazon
v.n. To be famous; to be notorious.

ety: for A. شہرة, inf. n. of شہر 'to be apparent, or conspicuous,' &c.
def: adj. Of world-wide celebrity, renowned
more: (from شہر To publish) s. f. 1. Renown, fame, celebrity. 2. Divulging, report, rumour, celebrating.
subs: (-kī), To acquire a reputation (for); to become famous
(-ko), To give publicity (to), to proclaim, to blazon
v.n. To be famous; to be notorious.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شُهْرَت (šuhrat), from Arabic شُهْرَة (šuhra). First attested in c. 1688 as Middle Hindi شہرت (śhrt).

ety: inf. n. of شہد 'to be present'
def: s.m. The being present; — adj. & s.m. Present; — one personally present.
more: (from شہد) 1. s. The being present. 2. adj. Present. 3. s. plur. (of شاهد) Witnesses, evidences. ¶ shahūd, Friday, the day of the resurrection.

def: corr. shahūd, s.m. pl. (of shāhid, q.v.), Witnesses; evidences, testimonies; — Friday; the day of resurrection.
s.m. pl. (of shahr, q.v.), Lunar months, months.

ety: v.n. fr. شجع 'to be or become brave,' &c.
def: adj. Courageous, brave, valiant, intrepid, bold, daring ( = diler).
more: (from شجع To be brave; Heb. To be mad, to rage. Orig. in impetuous action: Willmet ) adj. Brave. (Pers. plur. شجاعان shujāʼāﻌn, The brave).

def: s.f. = shujāʻat, q.v.
(for A. شقة shuqqat, v.n. fr. شقّ 'to tear, rend,' &c.), s.m. lit. 'A piece'; a royal order or communication, a letter (esp. from a superior), a note.
more: n. m. A royal order or communication from a king to his nobles, etc.
(from شقّ) s. m. A royal order, a letter (especially from a superiour).

def: See سکل پچهہ sukal-pachchh̤, under سکل sukal.
s.f. Majesty, magnificence, dignity, grandeur, state; train, pomp, parade; stir, to-do
more: (improp. shikoh ) s. f. Dignity, state, grandeur, pomp, parade.
subs: To act pompously, to make a stir, or to-do.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian شِکْوَه (šikwa), borrowed from Arabic شَكْوَة (šakwa), from شَكَا (šakā).

def: s.m. Thanks, gratitude; thanksgiving, praise (to God)
more: n. m. 1. ( sūkh ) The planet Venus. 2. Friday ( jumā ). Shukkar-vār kī bādlī rahī sanīchar chhāë, Aisā bole Bhaḍḍarī bin barse nahīṅ jāë . Prov. saying. A cloudy Friday which extends to Saturday Without rain, Bhaḍrī says, will never pass away. 3. ( Met .) A great gormandizer. Khāne meṅ shukkar hai . Prov. saying. He is a Shukr to eat (a great gormandizer). shukkar Sanīchar . 1. Friday and Saturday. 2. The names of two gluttons; hence, any great eater.

n. m. Thanks; gratitude. shukr-i-Khudā! Thank God! shukr-i-khidmat , n. m. An acknowledgment of a service. shukr k., bajā lānā, yā adā k. , v. a. To thank; return thanks; acknowledge a favor. Shālā shāttu khā-kar shukar kartā hai, Allā miyāṅ ko kharāb kartā hai! Bengalis. The rascal thanks God for gram meal and just spoils Him. (God will not give him better while he is thankful for worse). shukr-guzār , adj. Grateful; thankful. shukr-guzārī , n. f. Returning thanks; thanksgiving; gratitude; gratefulness. shukr-i-nemat . An acknowledgment of a favor, etc.

(Heb. To hire, to pay rent) s. m. Thanks, gratitude. شکرکرنا shukr karnā, To thank, to return thanks, to acknowledge a favour. شکرگذار shukr-guẕār, Grateful, thankful. شکرگذاري shukr-guẕār-ī, s. f. Thanksgiving, gratitude, thanks, thankfulness. شکرنعمت shukr-i-niﻌmat, An acknowledgment of favour. ¶ shakr, s. f. 1. Vulva. 2. Coitus.
subs: or shukr ba-jā lānā (-kā) = shukr karnā, q.v.
Thank God! God be praised! — shukr-ě-ḵẖidmat, s.m. An acknowledgment of service
(-kā), To return thanks (for), to thank, to be grateful (for); to acknowledge (a favour); to praise (God, for)

def: v.t. To water, irrigate; to lave; to rinse out; to empty (also written sīṅćnā).
more: v. a. S. सिञ्च, to water. 1. To irrigate ( pānī denā ). 1. Babā ghan baburī unchwā re! sīchan ham jaibo singrī kā . Song. Thick sow on high the accacia tree, I'll water it and reap its fruit. 2. Āg lage phūle phale, sīchat jāve sūkh, Maiṅ tohe pūchhūṅ, e sakhī, 'phūl ke bhītar rūkh?' Riddle. Ans. Ātish-bāzī kā anār . def: part. adj. Accomplished, effected, brought about, completed, finished, perfected, consummated; performed, fulfilled; realized; obtained, acquired, gained; successful; settled, established, substantiated, demonstrated, proved; admitted to be true or right; valid, sound, good; judged, determined, adjudicated, decided, terminated (as a law-suit, &c.); paid, liquidated, discharged, settled (as a debt, &c.); framed, formed, enacted, made (as a regulation, or an award, &c.); ready (as money, &c.), thoroughly prepared; made up, concocted, compounded (as drugs, &c.); cooked, dressed; matured, ripened, ripe; brought into subjection (by magical power); thoroughly skilled or versed (in science, or in magical arts, &c.); endowed with supernatural powers; perfected or sanctified (by penance, or austerities, &c.); emancipated, beatified; sacred, holy, pious, divine; immortal, eternal; well-known, eminent, celebrated; illustrious, shining, splendid; — s.m. A semi-divine being supposed to be of great purity and holiness, and said to be specially characterized by the eight siddhis or supernatural faculties (the Siddhas, together with the Vidyādharas, Munis, &c. are believed to inhabit the Bhuvarlok or middle region between the earth and the sun); — an inspired sage, a seer; a holy personage, a great saint, one who has subjected his will to the eight siddhis; — an ascetic who by mystical and austere practices has effected one or all of five purposes, viz. 1˚ the influence or wealth of the gods; 2˚ the form of the gods; 3˚ the society of the gods; 4˚ residence in the divine lokas; 5˚ identification with a deity; — an adept in magical or mystical arts, a magician, one who by the performance of certain mystical rites has acquired certain superhuman powers; — the twenty-first of the astronomical periods called yogas
more: 1. s. m. A class of demi-gods, inhabiting Indraʼs heaven. 2. A saint, a holy man, he who has subjected to his will the eight Siddhis: see اشٿسدهہ ash̤ṭa-siddhi. 3. adj. Successful. 4. Ready, completed, accomplished. 5. (for सिद्धि ) s. f. Success, accomplishment of an object. ¶ siddhi ( सिद्धि ) s. f. 1. Fulfilment, accomplishment, the entire completion of any undertaking or attainment of any object. 2. The result or fruit of the adoration of the gods, or of ascetick severities. 3. The supposed acquirement of supernatural powers by the completion of magical, mystical, or alchymical rites and processes. 4. Accuracy, correctness, indisputable conclusion or position.
subs: (˚dha+ar˚), adj. & s.m. Having attained (one's) aim, successful; — one who has accomplished his aim or object; — white mustard

(˚dha + an˚), s.m. Demonstrated conclusion, establisḥed truth, result, consequence, the determination or result of investigation or discussion, conclusion, decision; reliable doctrine; a system of science (esp. of astronomy, geometry, &c.), a common name of several treatises upon astronomy, astrology, algebra, and other sciences
s.m. The logically correct side of an argument
s.f. The point in an argument which is regarded as a logical conclusion

adj. Appertaining to a logical demonstration; — s.m. One who establishes or proves his conclusions, a logician; demonstrator; one learned in scientific text-books; a follower of the Mīmānsā philosophy

s.m. A perfect or completely sanctified personage, one who by abstraction and austerities has attained that degree of divine perfection which enables him to accomplish anything he desires; a sage, seer; an adept in mystical rites
adj. & s.m. Having perfected metallic fluids, mineral, metallic; — mercury, quicksilver
s.m. The performance of magical or mystical rites (for the acquisition of supernatural powers, &c.); the materials employed in mystical or chemical processes
s.m. An observer of the laws
(-ko), To carry into effect, to obey

def: s.f. Accomplishment, completion, perfection; success; validity (of a rule, &c.).

s.f. Accomplishment, fulfilment, consummation, completion, perfection, complete attainment (of any object), performance; success, prosperity; — settlement, establishment, substantiation, demonstration, proof, indisputable conclusion or position; validity (of a rule or law); — certainty, truth, accuracy, correctness; decision, adjudication, determination (of a lawsuit); payment, liquidation (of a debt), discharge, acquittance; — solution (of a problem); preparation, cooking, maturing, maturity; readiness; complete knowledge; understanding, intellect; complete perfection or sanctification (by penance or austerities); final emancipation, supreme felicity, beatitude; the acquisition of supernatural powers by magical means or through the performance of certain mystical or alchymical rites or processes; the supernatural faculty so acquired (eight are usually enumerated, viz. animan, mahiman, laghiman, gariman, prāpti, prākāmya, īśi-tva, vaśi-tva, qq.v.)
more: n. f. 1. Fulfilment; accomplishment. 2. Accuracy; indisputable conclusion or position. 3. The fruit of worship or asceticism; sanctification; perfection. 4. Final emancipation; beatitude. 5. The acquirement of supernatural powers by magic, mysticism, or alchemy ( asht sidahī ). 6. The hemp plant; Cannabis sativa .
s. (dakh.) Hemp: see Qanoon-e-islam, gloss. p. lxii and p. xcv.
subs: any marvellous skill or capability; concealment

vanishing, making oneself invisible; a magical shoe or slipper (supposed to have the power of conveying the wearer wherever he likes); — good effect or result, use, advantage; — name of a certain yoga (either the sixteenth of the astronomical periods or the nineteenth of the twenty-eight astrological yogas); — the hemp-plant, Cannabis sativa
s.m. Giver of perfection, &c.; conferrer of felicity or beatitude
s.f. (in Gram.) The junction of a consonant with a vowel
s.m. (f. -ī), One who possesses siddhi, q.v.

def: s.f. Straightness, directness; direct line, direction, aim (cf. sūdh); — adj. = sīdhā, q.v.

def: n. f. S. शुध्द 1. Straightness; directness. Nāk kī sīdh meṅ chale jāo . Follow your nose. 2. Aim. sīdh bāṅdhnā , v. a. 1. To lay out a line of road, etc. 2. To take aim. sīdh meṅ , adv. In a straight line.

adj. (f. -ī), Straight, direct, even; to the point; close (as a translation); point-blank; opposite, facing; erect, upright; accurate, correct, right, proper, excellent; plain, simple, artless; easy (to be accomplished, &c.); straightforward, honest, fair, candid; quiet, gentle, tractable; favourable, kind; right (hand); auspicious, lucky; — adv. Directly, straight, &c.; — s.m. Undressed victuals, provisions; forage; a settled allowance; — a charge made for provisions or supplies (to travellers, &c.); — sīdhā banānā (-ko), To make straight, to straighten; to set right, to regulate, to correct; to tame, train, teach( = sīdhā karnā)
more: adj. 1. Straight; direct. 1. Sīdhā ghar Khudā kā . Prov. Straight is the road to Heaven. (It is easy to beg or to die). 2. Auspicious; lucky. Sīdhe din the jo gaī chīz mil gaī . 3. Quiet; gentle; meek. Sīdhā jānvar . 4. ( sīdhā sādhā ) Artless ( bholā ). Sīdhā ādmī . 5. Right (opp. of left). 6. Correct; regular; according to rule. 7. Easy ( āsān ). Yeh to sīdhā savāl hai, is meṅ mushkil kyā? 8. Favorable; kind; friendly. 9. In good tilth (land). sīdhā , adv. Directly; straight. sīdhā ānā yā h. , v. n. To stand up to one; square at Woh to sidhā sir ko ātā hai. sīdhā banānā, yā k. , v. a. 1. To straighten; make straight. 2. See ṭhīk k. , 1—3 and ṭhīk banānā. Dekho! kaisā sīdhā banātā hūṅ! 3. To tame; train; teach. 4. ( sīdhā k .) To level (a gun); take aim. sīdhā sachchā , adj. Simple and straightforward. sīdhā hāth ; Sant. etom tī . The right hand. sīdhā h. , v. n. 1. To be straightened. …

n. m. S. असिद्ध unripe. 1. Provisions; uncooked victuals. Dayā-pāl, dātā jijmān, dinā duādshī, sīdhā dān! Mendicants' cry. O merciful and generous giver! twelfth-night alms bestow! 2. A settled allowance. 3. A charge made for supplies to travellers. sīdhā minasnā, chhūnā, nikālnā, yā pun k ., v. a. To set apart victuals for a Brahman.
1. adj. ( साधु ) Straight, right, direct. 2. Opposite. 3. Simple. 4. Fair, candid, upright. سيدها کرنا sīdhā karnā, To straighten. 5. s. m. (from सिद्ध ) Provisions, victuals (undressed), forage.

more: v. n. S. सिध् to go. 1. To go; depart; set out; start. Barsat nain hamāre, so nis din barsat nain hamāre, Sadā rahat barkhā rut ham par jab se Shiyām sidhāre . Sūr Dās. My eyes the rains, all night and day tears constant rain, The rains have ne'er relaxed since me hath left my swain. 2. To leave this world; to die.
v. n. To go, to depart, to set off.
wety: Derived from سدھار (sudhār), which is from Sanskrit *शुद्धकार (*śuddhakāra, “the act of purifying or removing errors”), from शुद्ध (śuddha) + कार (kāra) (compare Sanskrit शुद्धीकरोति (śuddhīkaroti), from शुद्धीकृ (śuddhīkṛ, “to purify, to remove errors”, root), from शुद्ध (śuddha, adjective) + ईकृ (īkṛ, root form)). Cognate with Punjabi ਸੁਧਾਰਨਾ (sudhārnā) / سُدھارنا (sudhārnā), Nepali सुधार्नु (sudhārnu), Bengali …

Bricks in a kiln. A husband see with nine lakh wives, all stretched upon his bed, The husband burns, the world looks on, his wives decked out instead. 3. Kadhī na kāto kātno, maiṅ ne mūsal so lāṛo tār. Kadhī na soī scj pe, māiṅ ne kūṭ bichhāī bār, Maiṅ albelī nār! mo se phūaṛ ko kahe? Rus. Wom. song. I never spun but threads as pestle thick I drew, On bed ne'er slept but thorns for mattress on it threw, I am a woman rare, who says I am a fool? 4. Dukh meṅ sumran sab kareṅ, sukh meṅ kare na koë, Jo sukh meṅ sumran kareṅ to dukh kā he ko hoë? Dohā. In sorrow all remember Him, in joy forget again, In joy if they remembered Him there never would be pain. 3. Pāṅch pahar dhande meṅ khoë, nām na let Hari kā, Tin pahar tiryā saṅg rāche, sukh mānā indri kā . Bhajan. Five mortal watches passed in toil, and ne'er a thought of Heaven, But three are left and these are all to sensual pleasure given. 4. Buḍḍhā byāh kare, paṛausiyoṅ ko sukh hove . Prov. When the old man takes a wife the neighbours have a pleasant life. 5. Sukh ko kīyā saneh, pāṛā dūkh dūnā . Prov. I loved that I might pleasure get, redoubled pain I did beget. sukh-bās, sukh-dhām , n. m. Abode of comfort or happiness. sukh-pāl , n. m. A kind of pālkī , q. v. sukh pānā , v. n.…

more: v. n. S. सिक्ष Pālī, sikhati . 1. To learn; acquire; pick up. 1. Kyā karāṅ! nā-chār Raṅgīṅ kī khushī ke vāste Sīkhtī maiṅ bhī chalī hūṅ ab to karnā ūhī! āh! Wom. Raṅgīṅ. Dont, daughter-in-law, this wanton mincing gait affect! from whom learnt you this gait? 3. Bagair sīkhe kuchh nahīṅ ātā . Prov. Saying. Nothing can be acquired without being taught. 2. To go to school; serve one's apprenticeship. īkhā-sīkhī , n. f. Emulation; rivalry.

wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀲𑀺𑀓𑁆𑀔𑀇 (sikkhaï), from Sanskrit शिक्ष॑ति (śíkṣati), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćíkšati, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱí-ḱₔk-se-ti, from *ḱek-.

def: s.m. = سکه sikka, q.v.
wety: Either from Classical Persian سکا (sakā) or a modern learned borrowing from an ancient Indo-Iranian language referring to the same nomadic peoples.

ety: for A. سکة sikkat, v.n. سكّ 'to clamp,' &c.
def: s.m. A stamp, seal, signet; a coining-die; type, printing-letter; — an impression on money; stamped coin, a rupee; sterling or current coin; name of a weight (equal, in Bengal, to 7 dwt. and 11·5511 grains); — adj. Stamped, sterling, current
more: n. 1. A stamp; seal; signet; royal signet. 2. An impression on money. 3. Sterling; current coin. sikka-i-aslī , G. G. Sterling coin. sikka biṭhānā , v. a. To establish one's rule, authority, etc. sikka banānā , v. a. To coin money. sikka paṛne kā ghar ; H. ṭaksāl , n. m. A mint. sikka-i-chehre-shāhī , n. m. The English Rupee. sikka-i-rāyaj-ul-waqt , n. m. Current coin; legal tender. sikka-zan ; H. ṭāksālyā , n. m. A coiner. sikka-zanī , n. f. Coining. sikka-sanad , A paper bearing the royal signet; a grant from the crown under sign-manual, or bearing the Ruler's seal. sikka-i-qadīm ; H. purānā rupayā , Old coin, sikka-i-qalab yā jālī . Counterfeit coin. ghisā sikka, yā rupayā , Rubbed coin.

(from سکّ To cover, particularly with a plate of iron. Heb. To cover. Talmud. To number or sum up money. A hiding place, a cottage, a nail or button, a coining die) s. m. 1. A coining die. 2. An impression on money. 3. Sterling, current. 4. Stampt coin.
subs: (apnā), 'To introduce (one's) coin,' &c., to establish (one's) rule or authority, &c.
s.m. New coin, coin recently stamped
s.f. A paper or document stamped with the royal seal; a grant from the crown under sign-manual or bearing the ruler's seal
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سِکَّه (sikka), from Arabic سِكَّة (sikka).

def: s.m. Weight, gravity, ponderousness; a weight; a load or burden, heavy load, indigestion.

s. m. 1. Gravity, weight. 2. Indigestion, heavy load.
